moment: I feel uncertain about what comes next, and I want to know where to stay.

passage.text: The next day again John was standing with two of his disciples, and he looked at Jesus as he walked by and said, "Behold, the Lamb of God!" The two disciples heard him say this, and they followed Jesus. Jesus turned and saw them following and said to them, "What are you seeking?" And they said to him, "Rabbi" (which means Teacher), "where are you staying?" He said to them, "Come and you will see." So they came and saw where he was staying, and they stayed with him that day, for it was about the tenth hour.

prayer:
Lord Jesus, You turn toward those who follow.
Teach me to ask honestly.
Give me grace to come, and patience to see.
Help me stay with You in this day.

notes: This is pastoral presence, not diagnosis.
Invite gently; do not pressure outcomes.
Keep questions invitational; do not corner the reader.
Do not rush from seeking to certainty; let the scene unfold.
Stay close to the passage's verbs: follow, turn, see, ask, come, stay.
